meta.chapter_name: A Tale of Three Intersecting Lines

questions.text: A triangle has how many sides, angles and vertices?
questions.explanation: A triangle has 3 sides, 3 angles and 3 vertices.

questions.text: The sum of all interior angles of a triangle is:
questions.explanation: The angle sum property: sum of all interior angles of any triangle = 180 degrees.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, angle A = 60 and angle B = 80 degrees. Angle C = ?
Two angles of a triangle are 55 and 75 degrees. The third angle is:
questions.explanation: 55 + 75 = 130. Third angle = 180 - 130 = 50 degrees.

questions.text: A triangle with angles 30, 60 and 90 degrees is a:
questions.explanation: One angle is exactly 90 degrees, so it is a right-angled triangle.

questions.text: An obtuse triangle has:
questions.explanation: An obtuse triangle has exactly one angle greater than 90 degrees.

questions.text: An equilateral triangle has each angle equal to:
questions.explanation: All three angles are equal and sum to 180. Each angle = 180/3 = 60 degrees.

questions.text: A triangle with two equal sides is called:
questions.explanation: Isosceles triangle: exactly two equal sides. The base angles (opposite equal sides) are also equal.

questions.text: A triangle with sides 5 cm, 5 cm and 5 cm is:
questions.explanation: All three sides equal = equilateral triangle.

questions.text: An isosceles triangle has base angles of 50 degrees each. The vertex angle is:
questions.explanation: Vertex angle = 180 - 50 - 50 = 80 degrees.

questions.text: The Exterior Angle Theorem states that the exterior angle of a triangle equals:

options.b: 180 degrees minus the largest interior angle
options.c: The sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles
options.d: Half the sum of all interior angles

questions.explanation: Exterior angle = sum of the two remote (non-adjacent) interior angles.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, angle A = 50 and angle B = 70. The exterior angle at C is:
questions.explanation: Exterior angle at C = angle A + angle B = 50 + 70 = 120 degrees.

questions.text: A median of a triangle is a line from a vertex to:

options.a: The midpoint of the opposite side
options.b: The foot of the perpendicular on the opposite side
options.d: The midpoint of an adjacent side

questions.explanation: A median goes from a vertex to the midpoint of the opposite side.

questions.text: How many medians does every triangle have?
questions.explanation: One median from each vertex. Since a triangle has 3 vertices, it has 3 medians.

questions.text: The point where all three medians of a triangle meet is called the:
questions.explanation: All three medians meet at the centroid (also called the centre of gravity).

questions.text: The altitude of a triangle is the perpendicular from a vertex to:

options.a: The midpoint of the opposite side

questions.explanation: The altitude is the perpendicular dropped from a vertex to the opposite side or its extension.

questions.text: The point where all three altitudes of a triangle meet is called the:
questions.explanation: All three altitudes meet at the orthocentre.

questions.text: Can a triangle have sides 3 cm, 4 cm and 8 cm?

options.a: Yes, any three lengths form a triangle
options.c: Yes, because 8 is the largest side

questions.explanation: Triangle inequality: sum of any two sides must be greater than the third. 3+4=7, which is NOT greater than 8. So triangle is impossible.

questions.text: Can a triangle be both right-angled and equilateral?

options.b: No, equilateral has all 60-degree angles, right angle requires one 90-degree angle

questions.explanation: Equilateral: all angles = 60 degrees. Right-angled: one angle = 90 degrees. These contradict each other. Impossible.

questions.text: The centroid divides each median in the ratio ___ from the vertex:
questions.explanation: The centroid divides each median in ratio 2:1 from the vertex. So vertex to centroid = 2/3 of the median.

questions.text: Two angles of a triangle are 47 and 63 degrees. The third angle is:
questions.explanation: Third angle = 180 - 47 - 63 = 70 degrees.

questions.text: In triangle PQR, the exterior angle at Q is 115 degrees and angle P = 55 degrees. Find angle R.
questions.explanation: Exterior angle = sum of non-adjacent interior angles: 115 = 55 + angle R. Angle R = 60 degrees.

questions.text: Can a triangle have sides 6 cm, 8 cm and 15 cm?

options.d: No, it must be a right triangle

questions.explanation: 6+8=14. Since 14 < 15, the triangle inequality fails. Not possible.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, AB = AC. If angle A = 40 degrees, find angle B.
questions.explanation: AB=AC means isosceles. Angle B = angle C. 40+2(angle B)=180. 2(angle B)=140. Angle B=70.

questions.text: The angles of a triangle are in ratio 1:2:3. Find all three angles.
questions.explanation: Let angles be x, 2x, 3x. x+2x+3x=6x=180. x=30. Angles: 30, 60, 90 degrees.

questions.text: In a right-angled triangle, one acute angle is 35 degrees. The other acute angle is:
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, two acute angles add to 90. Other = 90 - 35 = 55 degrees.

questions.text: The exterior angle of a triangle is 130 degrees. One non-adjacent interior angle is 70 degrees. The other non-adjacent angle is:
questions.explanation: Exterior = sum of two non-adjacent. 130 = 70 + other. Other = 60 degrees.

questions.text: The angles of a triangle are in ratio 3:4:5. Find the largest angle.
questions.explanation: 3k+4k+5k=12k=180. k=15. Largest=5x15=75 degrees.

questions.text: Can a triangle have sides 5 cm, 7 cm and 11 cm?

options.a: No, 5+7=12 is not greater than 11... wait

questions.explanation: Check smallest two sides: 5+7=12 > 11. Triangle inequality satisfied. Yes, possible.

questions.text: A median is 9 cm long. The centroid divides it in ratio 2:1 from vertex. Distance from vertex to centroid is:
questions.explanation: Vertex to centroid = 2/3 of median = 2/3 x 9 = 6 cm.

questions.text: Triangle ABC has angle A = 2x, angle B = 3x and angle C = 4x. Find x and classify the triangle by angles.
questions.explanation: 2x+3x+4x=9x=180. x=20. Angles: 40, 60, 80. All less than 90 = acute triangle.

questions.text: In triangle XYZ, exterior angle at Z = 3x + 10 and two non-adjacent interior angles are (x + 20) and (x + 30). Find x.
Angles of a triangle are in ratio 2:3:4. Find all three angles and classify the triangle.
questions.explanation: 2k+3k+4k=9k=180. k=20. Angles: 40, 60, 80. All less than 90, so acute triangle.

questions.text: In isosceles triangle ABC, AB = AC. The exterior angle at A is 140 degrees. Find angle B.
questions.explanation: Interior angle A = 180-140=40 degrees. Isosceles: angle B=angle C. 40+2B=180. 2B=140. B=70 degrees.

questions.text: In right triangle ABC, angle C = 90 degrees. If one median from C to midpoint M of AB = 6.5 cm, find AB.
questions.explanation: In a right triangle, the median to the hypotenuse = half the hypotenuse. So CM = AB/2. 6.5 = AB/2. AB = 13 cm.

questions.text: The angles of a quadrilateral are in ratio 1:2:3:4. Is the sum of the two middle angles the same as the sum of all angles of a triangle?

options.c: No, the middle angles sum to 216
options.d: Yes, but only for special quadrilaterals

questions.explanation: Quadrilateral sum=360. Ratio 1:2:3:4, total parts=10. Each part=36. Two middle angles=2x36+3x36=72+108=180. Triangle angle sum=180. Yes, both equal 180.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, angle A = 60 and angle B = 80 degrees. Find angle C.
In triangle ABC, angle A = 45 and angle B = 65. Find the exterior angle at C.
questions.explanation: Exterior angle at C = A + B = 45 + 65 = 110 degrees.

questions.text: The angles of a triangle are in ratio 2:3:4. Find the largest angle.
questions.explanation: 2k+3k+4k=9k=180. k=20. Largest=4x20=80 degrees.

questions.text: Angles of a triangle are in ratio 1:2:3. Find x (smallest angle).
questions.explanation: 6x=180. x=30 degrees.

questions.text: In triangle ABC, AB = AC and angle A = 40 degrees. Find angle B.
questions.explanation: Isosceles: angle B=angle C. 40+2B=180. B=70 degrees.

questions.text: In triangle XYZ, exterior angle at Z = 3x + 10 and non-adjacent angles are (x + 20) and (x + 30). Find x.
Triangle ABC has angle A = 2x, angle B = 3x, angle C = 4x. Find angle A.
questions.explanation: 9x=180. x=20. Angle A=2x=40 degrees.

questions.text: A median of a triangle is 12 cm. Find the distance from vertex to centroid.
questions.explanation: Centroid divides median in 2:1 from vertex. Vertex to centroid = 2/3 x 12 = 8 cm.

questions.text: The exterior angle of a triangle is 130 degrees. One non-adjacent interior angle is 70 degrees. Find the other non-adjacent interior angle.
questions.explanation: 130 = 70 + other. Other = 60 degrees.

questions.text: In an isosceles triangle, base angles are 50 degrees each. Find the vertex angle.
questions.explanation: Vertex angle = 180 - 50 - 50 = 80 degrees.

questions.case_text: An architect is designing a triangular roof. The roof is an isosceles triangle where the two equal sides make an angle of 40 degrees at the top (vertex angle = 40 degrees). The base of the triangle is 8 m wide and the median from the vertex to the base is 6 m long.

subparts.text: Each base angle of the isosceles triangular roof is:
subparts.explanation: Vertex = 40. Two base angles equal and sum = 180-40=140. Each = 70 degrees.

subparts.text: The centroid divides the median (6 m) from vertex to base in ratio 2:1. Distance from centroid to base midpoint is:
subparts.explanation: Centroid divides in 2:1 from vertex. Centroid to base = 1/3 of 6 = 2 m.

subparts.text: Is the triangular roof an acute, right or obtuse triangle?
subparts.explanation: Angles are 40, 70, 70. All less than 90 degrees. So it is an acute triangle.

questions.case_text: A surveyor is studying a triangular plot of land ABC. He finds that: angle A = 3y - 10 degrees, angle B = y + 30 degrees, angle C = 2y - 20 degrees. He also knows that the exterior angle at C is 2 times angle A.

subparts.text: Using the angle sum property, the equation is (3y-10)+(y+30)+(2y-20)=180. Find y.
Verify: Find the exterior angle at C using angle A + angle B.
subparts.explanation: Angle A=80, Angle B=y+30=60. Exterior at C=80+60=140 degrees.
